text: Fill in the blanks about the posterior triangle of the neck.
questions: The posterior triangle is bounded anteriorly by the *sternocleidomastoid/SCM:muscle that divides the neck into anterior and posterior triangles*, posteriorly by the trapezius, and inferiorly by the middle third of the *clavicle:bone forming the base of the triangle*.
Erb's palsy affects the upper trunk of the brachial plexus (C5, C6), resulting in the *waiter's tip:characteristic arm position with adducted shoulder and extended elbow* position.
The *accessory/spinal accessory:cranial nerve XI* nerve crosses the posterior triangle obliquely and is at risk during lymph node biopsy in this region.

Torticollis (wryneck) is caused by fibrosis of the *sternocleidomastoid/SCM:muscle affected in congenital torticollis* muscle, causing the head to tilt to the affected side with the chin rotated to the *opposite:direction the chin turns* side.
The brachial plexus roots emerge between the *scalenus anterior:anterior scalene muscle* and scalenus medius muscles in the posterior triangle.

overallFeedback.feedback: Keep studying — review the material and try again.
